nameMeanings.name: Frederick
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the ancient Germanic personal name Frideric or Frederic, composed of the elements frid, fred ‘peace’ + rīc ‘power’, introduced into England from France by the Normans. In North America, this is also found as an altered form of a cognate name in several other languages, such as German Friedrich and Czech Bedřich (see Bedrick ). Compare Fredrick .

variants.name: Frederic
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alphonse, Antoine, Andre, Armand, Eugenie, Leonne, Loubert.
French, West Indian (mainly Haiti), and Mauritian (Frédéric): from the personal name Frédéric, French form of Frederick .
French Canadian (Frédéric): altered form of German Friedrich .

variants.name: Freson
meanings: Walloon and French (also Fréson): nickname from Walloon (regional French) fréçon ‘friend’. Alternatively, perhaps a derivative of a short form of the personal name Frédéric (see Frederick ). This surname, which is also found in the Flemish part of Belgium (mainly Limburg), is rare in France.

variants.name: Friedrich
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Otto, Hans, Ernst, Ewald, Franz, Gerhard, Heiner, Heinz, Helmuth, Klaus, Reinhard.

German: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements frid, fred ‘peace’ + rīc ‘power’. The name was borne by a 9th-century Christian saint, bishop of Utrecht, and was a hereditary name among the Hohenstaufen ruling family; hence its popularity in central Europe.

variants.name: Fredrick
meanings: Altered form of English   Frederick   or like-sounding cognates in other languages, such as German   Friedrich  .
African (mainly Tanzania and Nigeria): from the personal name Fredrick, a shortened variant of English Frederick .

variants.name: Fred
meanings: Native American (e.g. Hopi): adoption of the English personal name Fred (short form of Frederick ) as a surname.
Possibly also an American shortened (and altered) form of English   Frederick   or any of its European cognates such as German   Friedrich  .

variants.name: Frigo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Gino, Amedeo, Antonio, Dario, Dino, Enio, Erminio, Reno, Renzo, Rinaldo, Ulisse, Vitina.  French Andre, Dominique.
Italian: from a short form of the personal name Federigo, an Italian form of Frederick .
French: variant of Frigault, from the personal name Frigowald, composed of the ancient Germanic elements fric ‘greedy, grasping’ + wald ‘rule, power’.

variants.name: Feick
meanings: North German: probably a derivative of the Frisian personal name Feiko, a pet form of Fre(de)rik (see Frederick ).

variants.name: Fredericks
meanings: English: variant of Frederick , with post-medieval excrescent -s.
Americanized form of German   Friedrichs   and possibly other related European surnames.

en.lifeSummary: Frederick I had at least 1 son.

fr.lifeSummary: Frederick I a eu au moins 1 fils.

it.lifeSummary: Frederick I lui avuto almeno 1 figlio.

ja.lifeSummary: Frederick Iは少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。

ko.lifeSummary: Frederick I(은)는 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Frederick I teve pelo menos 1 filho.

ru.lifeSummary: У Frederick I был по крайней мере 1 сын.

es.lifeSummary: Frederick I tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo.
